behaviors.description: Trim conversation history to the most recent 10 messages before sending to the LLM when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local trimming logic (count/order), not a cross-module-only bug. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: no dependence on model judgment. Rejection-gate demotion below Agentic proofs: deterministic plumbing quote: "trimmed to the most recent 10 messages"; excludes model-output-content quote: "when the full history exceeds the context window" (input shaping, not response quality). Higher levels rejected because behavior text targets preprocessing only, not DB/HTTP/model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the history-trimming function with >10 messages and assert exactly the latest 10 (correct order) are returned; include boundary cases at 10 and 11 to catch off-by-one and ordering regressions.

behaviors.description: Generated response should answer the customer’s actual question, not be generic or off-topic.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is explicitly about response relevance/quality, which depends on real model output and can vary run-to-run. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed responses can always look relevant and would hide real failures. MULTI-STEP: single model-quality decision, so Agentic not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompts representing customer questions against the production model path and evaluate relevance (rubric or human-labeled set). Fail when responses are generic/off-topic above threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects overly formal output for casual-tier users and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is a model-dependent tone judgment plus gate decision. LLM-DEP: "correctly identifies when a response is too formal" is output-quality classification, not just wiring. Integration rejected by brake (b)/(d): correctness depends on model judgment governing handoff to regeneration. Workflow rejected because behavior requires one agentic decision to trigger regeneration, not validating a full multi-agent pipeline quality end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model-generated candidate replies for casual-tier cases, run the real tone-check path, and assert overly formal outputs are flagged and regeneration call is invoked.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user message, response(s), regenerations) with correct timestamps in the database.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff between chat flow and persistence schema/transaction boundaries. REAL-INFRA: local DB integration is sufficient; no requirement for real external network/process. LLM-DEP excluded: target is storage completeness/timestamps, not response quality. Rejection-gate demotion below Agentic proofs: deterministic plumbing quote: "persisted to the database with correct timestamps"; excludes model-output-content quote: "full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations)" (recording artifacts regardless of semantic quality).
behaviors.test_description: Execute chat flow with a deterministic fake generator producing two responses (initial + regeneration), write via real repository/DB layer (test DB), then assert rows/events contain all messages and valid ordered timestamps.

behaviors.description: If regeneration is triggered, only the second response is returned to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is orchestration/state handoff across modules (draft state, replacement, response publisher). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network required. LLM-DEP excluded because precondition is explicit: "When the tone check triggers regeneration"; test target is visibility replacement, not model correctness. Rejection-gate demotion below Agentic proofs: deterministic plumbing quote: "the second response replaces the first"; excludes model-output-content quote: "the customer never sees the rejected draft." Workflow rejected: although sequence exists, this behavior validates one handoff policy, not multiple agentic decisions’ quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire controller/service/reply-delivery modules with deterministic stubs that force tone-trigger and provide first/second drafts; assert outbound customer payload contains only second draft and first is kept internal-only.
